title: veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. anagallidiformis
intro: Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. anagallidiformis, also known as Marsh Speedwell or Water Pimpernel, is a semi-aquatic perennial plant native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. anagallidiformis include reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and potentially lowering blood pressure. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine to create salves and creams for topical application.
